executiveSummary.summary: Solid technical foundation but facing three major headwinds: POS incumbents expanding into your space, a 3-month value gap that kills adoption, and selling to the most software-resistant market segment. The business is viable if you solve the cold-start problem and choose integration over competition.

marketSize.tam: $4.2B global restaurant inventory management software market (Grand View Research 2023)
marketSize.sam: $800M US independent restaurant inventory software market (650K independent restaurants × $1,200 annual spend)
marketSize.methodology: TAM from industry reports, SAM estimated from restaurant count × pricing, SOM assumes 0.15% market penetration

competitors.name: Toast
competitors.description: POS system adding inventory management features
competitors.strength: Already embedded in restaurant operations, massive distribution
competitors.weakness: Inventory is secondary feature, not core competency

marketAnalysis.positioning: The only AI-first inventory system built specifically for independent restaurants, not chains
marketAnalysis.timing: Post-COVID focus on margins + labor shortage driving automation needs
gaps: Cold-start ML problem unsolved by competitors
Independent restaurant-specific features missing from POS solutions

valueProposition.coreProblem: Independent restaurants waste 15-20% of food inventory due to poor demand forecasting and manual ordering processes
valueProposition.targetCustomer: Independent restaurant owners with $500K-$2M annual revenue, 2-5 locations max, currently using spreadsheets or basic POS inventory
valueProposition.uniqueAngle: ML predictions that factor weather, local events, and seasonal patterns - not just historical sales
valueProposition.whyNow: Rising food costs (up 25% since 2020) + labor shortage making manual inventory management unsustainable

businessModel.revenueStrategy: SaaS subscription with potential transaction fees on automated supplier orders
businessModel.pricingFramework: $199/month justified by saving 10% on $15K monthly food costs = $1,500 monthly savings
businessModel.scalabilityAssessment: Good margins once built, but high touch sales model limits scaling without enterprise pricing

technicalFeasibility.recommendedStack: Python/Django backend, React frontend, PostgreSQL, AWS hosting, ML pipeline with scikit-learn/TensorFlow

buildVsBuy.rationale: Use existing APIs rather than building connectors from scratch
Core differentiation, need custom algorithms for restaurant-specific factors
Partner with existing procurement platforms like Sysco or US Foods

technicalRisks: POS API rate limits and reliability
ML model accuracy with limited data
technicalFeasibility.estimatedMvpTimeline: 4-5 months for production-ready version with 2-person team

risks.description: Toast and Square bundling inventory features makes standalone solution less attractive
risks.mitigation: Focus on advanced ML capabilities they can't match, or become their AI layer via partnership

risks.description: 3-month cold start period before ML shows value kills adoption in price-sensitive market
risks.mitigation: Develop non-ML value props for first 90 days: waste tracking, supplier price comparison, basic reorder alerts

risks.description: Restaurants are notoriously difficult to sell to and retain
risks.mitigation: Partner with restaurant consultants, equipment vendors, or accounting firms for distribution

risks.description: POS integration complexity and maintenance burden
risks.mitigation: Start with 1-2 POS systems, use standardized APIs where possible

week1to4: Interview all 3 pilot customers about POS integration preferences
Build basic waste tracking features for immediate value

decisionAgenda.question: Integration strategy with POS incumbents

options.tradeoff: Faster adoption vs. margin compression and platform risk
Higher margins vs. much harder customer acquisition

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Integrate first - the distribution advantage outweighs margin concerns at this stage

decisionAgenda.question: Hero metric: waste reduction vs. cost savings

options.tradeoff: Easier to measure and prove vs. lower dollar impact
Higher ROI story vs. harder to attribute and prove

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Lead with waste reduction (measurable, immediate) but convert to cost savings story for retention

decisionAgenda.question: Cold-start problem solution

options.option: Build non-ML features for first 90 days
options.tradeoff: Immediate value vs. development resource allocation
Faster ML value vs. less accurate predictions

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Hybrid approach: basic benchmarks + immediate waste tracking features
